summary: Done: replaced Features.update() body with deep-merge helpers (_update_value/_as_mergeable_dicts/_deep_merge_dicts) added right after the update method (around line 1248-1280); original update() signature/behavior for non-overlapping keys unchanged, so should not conflict with your decorator work.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator; applied to __init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear. My update() body is now just 'super().update(iterable, **kwds)' plus the decorator recomputing _column_requires_decoding after. If your deep-merge update() lands in the same file, wrap your update() body with @keep_features_dicts_synced too so decoding stays in sync after merges.
